profile.name: Dr. R.C. Malo
profile.headline: Designing inclusive interfaces & reproducible research
profile.bio: I'm Dr. R.C.Malo — I build usable systems, publish reproducible work, and teach students how to design responsibly. This portfolio showcases my projects, publications, and teaching.

hiring.title: Open to Collaboration & Hiring
hiring.description: I'm actively seeking research collaborators, postdoctoral fellows, and motivated graduate students interested in human-computer interaction, accessibility, and reproducible research methods. If you share a passion for inclusive design and rigorous experimentation, let's build something meaningful together.

lab.title: Advanced Systems & Learning Lab
lab.description: Our lab focuses on building usable systems, publishing reproducible work, and designing responsibly. We explore the intersection of human-computer interaction, machine learning, and accessibility to solve real-world problems. We prioritize hands-on projects, rigorous experiments, and open-source contributions.

projects.title: Assistive UX Toolkit
projects.subtitle: Tooling & patterns for inclusive interfaces
projects.description: A toolkit for designers & developers to build accessible UI components and test assistive workflows.

projects.title: Repro Pipelines
projects.subtitle: Workflows for reproducible experiments
projects.description: Automated scripts and containerized environments that make experiments reproducible.

projects.title: Study Portal
projects.subtitle: Participant recruitment & consent
projects.description: A secure, privacy-preserving portal to manage research participants and consent forms.

teaching.title: Human-Computer Interaction
teaching.description: Advanced study of human-computer interaction principles, interface design, and user experience research methods. Topics include cognitive psychology, accessibility, design thinking, and evaluation techniques.

teaching.title: User Interface Design
teaching.description: Introduction to designing intuitive and accessible user interfaces. Covers design principles, prototyping, usability testing, and modern UI frameworks.

teaching.title: Accessibility and Inclusive Design
teaching.description: Deep dive into designing technology for diverse users including those with disabilities. Explores WCAG guidelines, assistive technologies, and universal design principles.

teaching.title: Research Methods in Computing
teaching.description: Introduction to empirical research methods in computer science. Covers experimental design, statistical analysis, reproducibility, and scientific writing.

teaching.title: Advanced Topics in HCI
teaching.description: Seminar course exploring cutting-edge research in human-computer interaction. Students read and discuss recent papers, present research, and develop project proposals.

experience.title: Assistant Professor
experience.organization: University of Utah — Dept. of Civil Engineering
experience.description: Leading research in HCI, accessibility, and reproducible methods. Teaching undergraduate and graduate courses.

experience.title: Postdoctoral Researcher
experience.organization: National Institute of Standards — HCI Division
experience.description: Developed accessibility evaluation frameworks and published 4 journal papers on inclusive interface design.

experience.title: Research Assistant
experience.organization: University of Utah — Interactive Systems Lab
experience.description: Contributed to 3 major research projects on assistive technology and user study design.

education.title: Ph.D. in Human-Computer Interaction
education.description: Dissertation: "Designing Inclusive Pipelines for Reproducible HCI Research." Advised by Prof. Example.

education.title: M.S. in Computer Science
education.description: Specialization in AI and Human Factors. Thesis on adaptive user interfaces.

education.title: B.S. in Cognitive Science
education.description: Minor in CS. Graduated with honors. Capstone on accessibility in mobile apps.
